headline: Dashboard live with ICARUS method (p=.031, Sharpe 1.25); FVG×corridor round 1 yielded no tradeable candidate; four hypotheses adjudicated below floor.
what_happened: Sharpe corrected from 1.08 to 1.25 (provisional, pending TICK-024); dashboard merges ICARUS shadow P&L calendar live with 16:20 auto-sync.
ICARUS (HYP-093) sealed: +0.023%/day constitutional yield, p=.031, DSR 0.987, but below your 0.05%/day floor; 3W/24L live, gates stand.
TICK-035 R1: 720 FVG×corridor cells mined 2018–Jun2024 (MNQ notional costs every fill); best cell +0.025%/day, negative median event, no preregister-worthy candidate.
HYP-097 refuted: W* collar physics dominate sub-$3 tier; constitutional yield fell to 0.0166%/day vs your 0.023% assumption; distribution sizing and edge concentration remain honest paths.
HYP-091, -094, -095 all not significant or below floor; HYP-096 withdrawn (unverified rule, no filesystem evidence).
what_it_means: You have a statistically sound fade method (ICARUS, p=.031) now live and feeding the dashboard daily, but its yield is ~2.3× below your 0.05%/day constitutional floor—same problem across the four adjudicated hypotheses. The FVG×corridor structure hunt yielded nothing; your sizing and concentration hypotheses are the remaining honest levers to reach the floor. The holdout (2024–26) stays virgin and your trial count stands.
todays_one_thing: Review TICK-035 R2 candidate structures (HTF FVGs, corridor-break events, gap clusters, overnight sessions) and lock in your pick; parallel: run a distribution-based sizing test (RCK or W6) on ICARUS to see if position scaling alone can close the yield gap to 0.05%/day.
